summary: German photographer (born 1955), student of Bernd and Hilla Becher. Known for mural-sized, large-format photographs often taken from high vantage points, extensively digitally edited to create repetitive patterns and surreal, expanded landscapes. His photograph Rhein II (a near-abstract image of the Rhine River between bands of grass and sky) is the most expensive photograph ever sold ($4.3 million in 2011). Also known for 99 Cent (and 99 Cent II Diptychon), showing aisles of a discount supermarket, and the Pyongyang series. Associated with the Düsseldorf School.

works.name: Rhein II (1999)
works.description: Gursky's most famous photograph — a near-abstract depiction of the Rhine River showing two horizontal bands of grass (one above, one below) separated by the gray-green river under a gray sky. Digitally edited to remove factories from the horizon. Sold for $4.3 million in 2011 — the most expensive photograph ever sold at auction. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Near-abstract image of the Rhine between two horizontal bands of grass and a gray sky; sold for $4.3 million in 2011 — the most expensive photograph ever sold; factories digitally removed from the horizon
examples: This photographer showed alternating bands of gray and green in his shot of a riverbank that ranks as the most expensive photograph ever sold.

This photographer, who created the Pyongyang series, made a color print mounted to glass that depicts two horizontal patches of grass, which are both bordered by a river; that work Rhein II is the most expensive photograph ever sold.

clues.clue: The digital editing removed large factories from the horizon
examples: That picture depicts this German river and is numbered 'II'; Gursky's editing removed the large factories on the horizon.

works.name: 99 Cent and 99 Cent II Diptychon (1999, 2001)
works.description: 99 Cent (1999) shows brightly colored, neatly organized rows of commercial goods on shelves in a discount California supermarket; the title word appears on the wall next to 'thanks,' 'only,' and 'nothing over.' 99 Cent II Diptychon (2001) is a two-panel version of a similar scene with hyper-saturated fluorescent lighting. The diptych was the fourth most expensive photograph ever sold. Both are mural-sized. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Shows brightly colored rows of products in a discount California supermarket; title word appears on the wall next to 'thanks,' 'only,' and 'nothing over'
examples: This man took a 1999 image, depicting people browsing fluorescently lit aisles, in which the title word appears on the walls upper next to the words 'thanks,' 'only,' and 'nothing over.'
This photographer showed aisles of products at a California superstore in his 99 Cent series.

clues.clue: 99 Cent II Diptychon is a two-panel version with hyper-saturated colors; was the fourth most expensive photograph ever sold
examples: A 2001 image of a similar scene is presented as a diptych and is the fourth-most-expensive photograph ever sold.
This artist's 99 Cent II Diptychon depicts rows of colorful products in a supermarket.

works.name: Pyongyang Series (2007)
works.description: Series of photographs taken at the Arirang Festival (Mass Games) in Pyongyang, North Korea. Shows thousands of cheerleaders in matching costumes with paneled depictions of flowers and doves in the background. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Shows thousands of cheerleaders in matching costumes at the Arirang Festival in Pyongyang, with paneled depictions of flowers and doves in the background
examples: Paneled depictions of flowers and doves appear in the background of thousands of cheerleaders in this artist's Pyongyang series.
Another photograph by this artist depicts over a thousand women wearing cheerleading costumes in front of a panorama of flowers, and was made at the Arirang Festival.

works.name: Stock Exchange Series and Other Works
works.description: Series of photographs depicting stock exchanges in Tokyo, Chicago, Hong Kong, Kuwait, New York, and other cities from aerial/high vantage points, using camera blur to capture movement. Chicago Board of Trade II is one example. Other works include: Bahrain I (Formula One racetrack in the desert); a photograph of the Super-Kamiokande neutrino observatory (showing golden spheres/phototubes); and a photograph of an Italian beach with hundreds of umbrellas grouped in colorful sections. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Series of aerial photos of stock exchanges (Tokyo, Chicago, Hong Kong, etc.) using blur to capture movement; includes Chicago Board of Trade II
examples: Two series of claustrophobic aerial photographs by this artist depict stock exchanges in Tokyo and Chicago.
This artist incorporated camera blur to capture movement in images of the Hong Kong, Tokyo, Kuwait, New York, and Chicago stock exchanges.

clues.clue: Bahrain I shows the auto racetrack of the Sakhir Grand Prix as a black tangle in the desert landscape
examples: The terrain of Bahrain's Sakhir Grand Prix is shown in one of this man's many photographs related to Formula One.
In a photograph by this artist, the asphalt of an auto racetrack forms a messy black tangle that stands out in the desert landscape.

clues.clue: Photograph of the Super-Kamiokande neutrino observatory showing golden spheres (phototubes) and two small humans on boats
examples: A wall of gold orbs features in this artist's photograph of the Super-Kamiokande observatory.
A photograph by this artist, which shows two humans on boats at its bottom, depicts hundreds of phototubes as golden spheres, and shows the Super Kamiokande reactor.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: German photographer (born 1955 in Leipzig). Student of Bernd and Hilla Becher (the Düsseldorf School). Known for large-format, mural-sized photographs extensively digitally edited. Photographs often taken from high vantage points showing repetitive patterns and crowds. Other works include Politik II (showing 13 politicians in conversation under a clock from an Ed Ruscha painting), a hotel interior combining three-floor shots into a rippling yellow light pattern, and a photograph of an Italian beach. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Student of Bernd and Hilla Becher; associated with the Düsseldorf School
examples: A photograph by this student of Bernd and Hilla Becher depicts two horizontal strips of grass under a gray sky.
For 10 points, Andreas Gursky was from what country home to the Düsseldorf School?

clues.clue: Politik II shows 13 politicians in conversation under a clock superimposed from an Ed Ruscha painting
examples: This photographer depicted 13 politicians having conversations under a clock superimposed from an Ed Ruscha painting.

comprehensive_summary:
Andreas Gursky (born 1955) is a German photographer and student of Bernd and Hilla Becher, associated with the Düsseldorf School. He is known for large-format, mural-sized photographs extensively digitally edited to create repetitive patterns, surreal compositions, and expanded perspectives, often taken from high vantage points.

His most famous work, Rhein II (1999), is a near-abstract image of the Rhine River showing two bands of green grass separated by the river under a gray sky — factories were digitally removed from the horizon. It sold for $4.3 million in 2011, the most expensive photograph ever sold at auction. His 99 Cent (1999) shows the brightly colored, neatly organized shelves of a California discount supermarket, with the title word appearing on the wall alongside 'thanks,' 'only,' and 'nothing over.' The two-panel 99 Cent II Diptychon (2001) is a hyper-saturated version and was the fourth most expensive photograph ever sold.

Gursky's Pyongyang series (2007) documents the Arirang Festival in North Korea, showing thousands of matching cheerleaders with paneled depictions of flowers and doves behind them. He made a series of aerial photographs of stock exchanges (Tokyo, Chicago, Hong Kong, Kuwait, New York) using blur to capture movement; Chicago Board of Trade II is one example. His Bahrain I shows the Sakhir Grand Prix racetrack as a black tangle in the desert landscape. He also photographed the Super-Kamiokande neutrino observatory, showing golden spheres (phototubes) reflected in the water with two humans on boats. Politik II depicts 13 politicians in conversation under a clock from an Ed Ruscha painting.
recursive_suggestions: Bernd and Hilla Becher — Gursky's teachers; the Düsseldorf School; associated with industrial typological photography
Cindy Sherman — repeatedly appears in the same bonus parts as Gursky for most expensive photographs
Düsseldorf School — the broader movement; could search for specific members

Photograph: shows thousands of matching cheerleaders with paneled depictions of flowers and doves behind them, taken at the Arirang Festival in Pyongyang
Photograph: shows the Super-Kamiokande neutrino observatory as golden spheres (phototubes) reflected in water, with two small humans on boats
